findings.location: s3 — コマンドライン起動引数の扱い
findings.description: The concrete bash code example showing the full java launch command, and the JavaScript-style output examples for getParamMap() and getArgs(), are omitted. These demonstrate how the three argument categories (JVM params, system properties, class args) are structured in a real command, and what values the API methods return for a given invocation. The textual format descriptions are present, but the examples provide additional clarity on the boundary between argument types.
findings.source_evidence: Source contains a `.. code-block:: bash` block with the full launch command including `-server`, `-Xmx128m`, `-DcommitInterval=100`, `-requestPath admin.DataUnloadBatchAction/BC0012`, etc., and two `.. code-block:: javascript` blocks showing sample return values of `Commandline#getParamMap()` and `Commandline#getArgs()`.
